volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 1.595 - Section 755)

sections.label: Section 1.595
sections.original: πήματα φθιτῶν ἐπὶ πήμασι πίπτοντʼ ,
sections.translation: are heaped upon the sorrows of the dead . Each generation does not set its race free , but some god hurls it down and the race has no release . For now that dazzling ray of hope that had been spread

sections.label: Section 1.600
sections.original: ῥίζας ὃ τέτατο φάος ἐν Οἰδίπου δόμοις ,
sections.translation: over the last roots in the house of Oedipus — that hope , in its turn , the blood-stained dust of the gods infernal and mindlessness in speech and frenzy at the mind cuts down .

sections.label: Section 2.605
sections.original: τεάν , Ζεῦ , δύνασιν τίς ἀνδρῶν ὑπερβασία κατάσχοι ;
sections.translation: Your power , great Zeus — what human overstepping can check it ? Yours is power that neither Sleep , the all-ensnaring , nor the untiring months of the gods can defeat . Unaged through time ,

sections.label: Section 2.610
sections.original: δυνάστας κατέχεις Ὀλύμπου μαρμαρόεσσαν αἴγλαν .
sections.translation: you rule by your power and dwell thereby in the brilliant splendor of Olympus . And through the future , both near and distant , as through the past , shall this law prevail : nothing that is vast comes to the life of mortals without ruin .

sections.label: Section 2.615
sections.original: ἁ γὰρ δὴ πολύπλαγκτος ἐλπὶς πολλοῖς μὲν ὄνασις ἀνδρῶν ,
sections.translation: See how that hope whose wanderings are so wide truly is a benefit to many men , but to an equal number it is a false lure of light-headed desires . The deception comes to one who is wholly unawares until he burns his foot on a hot fire .

sections.label: Section 2.620
sections.original: προσαύσῃ . σοφίᾳ γὰρ ἔκ του κλεινὸν ἔπος πέφανται .
sections.translation: For with wisdom did someone once reveal the maxim , now famous , that evil at one time or another seems good , to him whose mind a god leads to ruin .

sections.label: Section 2.625
sections.original: πράσσει δʼ ὀλίγιστον χρόνον ἐκτὸς ἄτας .
sections.translation: But for the briefest moment such a man fares free of destruction .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: ὅδε μὴν Αἵμων , παίδων τῶν σῶν
sections.translation: But here is Haemon , the last of your offspring . Does he come grieving for the doom of Antigone , his promised bride ,

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ἀπάτης λεχέων ὑπεραλγῶν ;
sections.translation: and bitter for the deceived hope of their marriage ?

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: τάχʼ εἰσόμεσθα μάντεων ὑπέρτερον .
sections.translation: We will soon know better than seers could tell us .— My son , can it be that after hearing the final judgment concerning your betrothed , you have come in rage against your father ? Or do I have your loyalty , act how I may ?

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: πάτερ , σός εἰμι , καὶ σύ μοι γνώμας ἔχων
sections.translation: Father , I am yours , and you keep me upright with precepts good for me — precepts I shall follow . No marriage will be deemed by me more important than your good guidance .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: γνώμης πατρῴας πάντʼ ὄπισθεν ἑστάναι .
sections.translation: Yes , my son , this is the spirit you should maintain in your heart — to stand behind your father ’ s will in all things . It is for this that men pray : to sire and raise in their homes children who are obedient , that they may requite their father ’ s enemy with evil and honor his friend , just as their father does .

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ὅστις δʼ ἀνωφέλητα φιτύει τέκνα ,
sections.translation: But the man who begets unhelpful children — what would you say that he has sown except miseries for himself and abundant exultation for his enemies ? Never , then , my son , banish your reason for pleasure on account of a woman ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ψυχρὸν παραγκάλισμα τοῦτο γίγνεται ,
sections.translation: knowing that this embrace soon becomes cold and brittle — an evil woman to share your bed and home . For what wound could strike deeper than a false friend ? No , spit her out as if she were an enemy , let her go find a husband in Hades .

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ἐπεὶ γὰρ αὐτὴν εἷλον ἐμφανῶς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: For since I caught her alone of all the city in open defiance , I will not make myself a liar to my city . I will kill her . So let her call on Zeus who protects kindred blood . If I am to foster my own kin to spurn order ,

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ἄκοσμα θρέψω , κάρτα τοὺς ἔξω γένους
sections.translation: surely I will do the same for outsiders . For whoever shows his excellence in the case of his own household will be found righteous in his city as well . But if anyone oversteps and does violence to the laws , or thinks to dictate to those in power ,

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐπαίνου τοῦτον ἐξ ἐμοῦ τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: such a one will never win praise from me . No , whomever the city may appoint , that man must be obeyed in matters small and great and in matters just and unjust . And I would feel confident that such a man would be a fine ruler no less than a good and willing subject ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: δορός τʼ ἂν ἐν χειμῶνι προστεταγμένον
sections.translation: and that beneath a hail of spears he would stand his ground where posted , a loyal and brave comrade in the battle line . But there is no evil worse than disobedience . This destroys cities ; this overturns homes ; this breaks

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: τροπὰς καταρρήγνυσι · τῶν δʼ ὀρθουμένων
sections.translation: the ranks of allied spears into headlong rout . But the lives of men who prosper upright , of these obedience has saved the greatest part . Therefore we must defend those who respect order , and in no way can we let a woman defeat us . It is better to fall from power , if it is fated , by a man ’ s hand ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: κοὐκ ἂν γυναικῶν ἥσσονες καλοίμεθʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: than that we be called weaker than women .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: ἡμῖν μέν , εἰ μὴ τῷ χρόνῳ κεκλέμμεθα ,
sections.translation: To us , unless our years have stolen our wit , you seem to say what you say wisely .

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: πάτερ , θεοὶ φύουσιν ἀνθρώποις φρένας ,
sections.translation: Father , the gods implant reason in men , the highest of all things that we call our own .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ὅπως σὺ μὴ λέγεις ὀρθῶς τάδε ,
sections.translation: For my part , to state how you are wrong to say those things is beyond my power and my desire , although another man , too , might have a useful thought . In any case , it is my natural duty to watch on your behalf all that men say , or do , or find to blame .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ σὸν ὄμμα δεινὸν ἀνδρὶ δημότῃ
sections.translation: For dread of your glance forbids the ordinary citizen to speak such words as would offend your ear . But I can hear these murmurs in the dark , how the city moans for this girl , saying : No woman ever merited death less —

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: κάκιστʼ ἀπʼ ἔργων εὐκλεεστάτων φθίνει .
sections.translation: none ever died so shamefully for deeds so glorious as hers , who , when her own brother had fallen in bloody battle , would not leave him unburied to be devoured by savage dogs , or by any bird . Does she not deserve to receive golden honor ?

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: τοιάδʼ ἐρεμνὴ σῖγʼ ἐπέρχεται φάτις .
sections.translation: Such is the rumor shrouded in darkness that silently spreads . For me , father , no treasure is more precious than your prosperity . What , indeed , is a nobler ornament for children than the fair fame of a thriving father , or for a father than that of his children ?

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: μή νυν ἓν ἦθος μοῦνον ἐν σαυτῷ φόρει ,
sections.translation: Do not , then , bear one mood only in yourself : do not think that your word and no other , must be right . For if any man thinks that he alone is wise — that in speech or in mind he has no peer — such a soul , when laid open , is always found empty .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἄνδρα , κεἴ τις ᾖ σοφός , τὸ μανθάνειν
sections.translation: No , even when a man is wise , it brings him no shame to learn many things , and not to be too rigid . You see how the trees that stand beside the torrential streams created by a winter storm yield to it and save their branches , while the stiff and rigid perish root and all ?

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: αὕτως δὲ ναὸς ὅστις ἐγκρατῆ πόδα
sections.translation: And in the same way the pilot who keeps the sheet of his sail taut and never slackens it , upsets his boat , and voyages thereafter with his decking underwater . Father , give way and allow a change from your rage . For if even from me , a younger man , a worthy thought may be supplied ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: πρόσεστι , φήμʼ ἔγωγε πρεσβεύειν πολὺ
sections.translation: by far the best thing , I believe , would be for men to be all-wise by nature . Otherwise — since most often it does not turn out that way — it is good to learn in addition from those who advise you well .

sections.label: Section 724
sections.original: ἄναξ , σέ τʼ εἰκός , εἴ τι καίριον λέγει ,
sections.translation: My king , it is right , if he speaks something appropriate , that you should learn from him

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: μαθεῖν , σέ τʼ αὖ τοῦδʼ · εὖ γὰρ εἴρηται διπλῇ .
sections.translation: and that you , in turn , Haemon , should learn from your father . On both sides there have been wise words .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: οἱ τηλικοίδε καὶ διδαξόμεσθα δὴ
sections.translation: Men of my age — are we , then , to be schooled in wisdom by men of his ?

sections.label: Section 728
sections.original: μηδὲν τὸ μὴ δίκαιον · εἰ δʼ ἐγὼ νέος ,
sections.translation: Not in anything that is not right . But if I am young , you should look to my conduct , not to my years .

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἔργον γάρ ἐστι τοὺς ἀκοσμοῦντας σέβειν ;
sections.translation: Is it worthy conduct to honor disrupters ?

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν κελεύσαιμʼ , εὐσεβεῖν εἰς τοὺς κακούς .
sections.translation: I could not urge anyone to show respect for the wicked .

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: οὐχ ἥδε γὰρ τοιᾷδʼ ἐπείληπται νόσῳ ;
sections.translation: And is she not in the grasp of that disease ?

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: οὔ φησι Θήβης τῆσδʼ ὁμόπτολις λεώς .
sections.translation: All the people of this city of Thebes deny it .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: πόλις γὰρ ἡμῖν ἁμὲ χρὴ τάσσειν ἐρεῖ ;
sections.translation: Shall Thebes prescribe to me how I must rule ?

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ὁρᾷς τόδʼ ὡς εἴρηκας ὡς ἄγαν νέος ;
sections.translation: See , there , how you have spoken so much like a child .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: ἄλλῳ γὰρ ἢ ʼμοὶ χρή με τῆσδʼ ἄρχειν χθονός ;
sections.translation: Am I to rule this land by the will of another than myself ?

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: πόλις γὰρ οὐκ ἔσθʼ ἥτις ἀνδρός ἐσθʼ ἑνός .
sections.translation: That is no city , which belongs to one man .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: οὐ τοῦ κρατοῦντος ἡ πόλις νομίζεται ;
sections.translation: Does not the city by tradition belong to the man in power ?

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: καλῶς γʼ ἐρήμης ἂν σὺ γῆς ἄρχοις μόνος .
sections.translation: You would make a fine monarch in a desert .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ὅδʼ , ὡς ἔοικε , τῇ γυναικὶ συμμαχεῖ .
sections.translation: This boy seems to be fighting on the side of the woman .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: εἴπερ γυνὴ σύ . σοῦ γὰρ οὖν προκήδομαι .
sections.translation: If you are a woman , for , to be sure , my concern is for you .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ὦ παγκάκιστε , διὰ δίκης ἰὼν πατρί ;
sections.translation: You traitor , attacking your father , accusing him !

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ δίκαιά σʼ ἐξαμαρτάνονθʼ ὁρῶ .
sections.translation: Because I see you making a mistake and committing injustice .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: ἁμαρτάνω γὰρ τὰς ἐμὰς ἀρχὰς σέβων ;
sections.translation: Am I making a mistake when I respect my own prerogatives ?

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ σέβεις , τιμάς γε τὰς θεῶν πατῶν .
sections.translation: Yes . You do not respect them , when you trample on the gods ’ honors .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: ὦ μιαρὸν ἦθος καὶ γυναικὸς ὕστερον .
sections.translation: Polluted creature , submitting to a woman !

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: οὔ τἂν ἕλοις ἥσσω γε τῶν αἰσχρῶν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: You will never catch me submitting to shamelessness .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: ὁ γοῦν λόγος σοι πᾶς ὑπὲρ κείνης ὅδε .
sections.translation: You do . Your every word , after all , pleads her case .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: καὶ σοῦ γε κἀμοῦ , καὶ θεῶν τῶν νερτέρων .
sections.translation: And yours , and mine , and that of gods below .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ταύτην ποτʼ οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὡς ἔτι ζῶσαν γαμεῖς .
sections.translation: You can never marry her , not while she is still alive .

sections.label: Section 751
sections.original: ἣ δʼ οὖν θανεῖται καὶ θανοῦσʼ ὀλεῖ τινα .
sections.translation: Then she will die , and in death destroy another .

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: ἦ κἀπαπειλῶν ὧδʼ ἐπεξέρχει θρασύς ;
sections.translation: What ! Does your audacity run to open threats ?

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἔστʼ ἀπειλὴ πρὸς κενὰς γνώμας λέγειν ;
sections.translation: How is it a threat to speak against empty plans ?

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: κλαίων φρενώσεις , ὢν φρενῶν αὐτὸς κενός .
sections.translation: You will regret your unwise instructions in wisdom .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: εἰ μὴ πατὴρ ἦσθʼ , εἶπον ἄν σʼ οὐκ εὖ φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: If you were not my father , I would have called you insane .
